issues.title: Sequential awaits for independent DB queries in fetchReceivedOrders
issues.description: The own-orders query (line 166) and the delegated-orders fetch (line 172) are independent and run sequentially. They can be parallelized with Promise.all to reduce total latency.
issues.impact: Two independent Supabase round-trips run sequentially; parallelizing them halves the DB wait time for this critical path.

issues.title: Array sort on every render without useMemo
issues.description: const sorted = [...section.items].sort(...) is computed on every render. If AccordionSection re-renders due to a parent state change (e.g. theme, locale), the sort runs again even if section.items hasn't changed. Same pattern exists in GallerySection (line 16), TwoColumnSection (line 17), and CardsSection (line 19).
issues.impact: Low in isolation (product sections are small), but these components are rendered several times per product-detail page. The fix is trivial and prevents unnecessary allocations.

issues.title: Top-level sections sort computed on every render without useMemo
issues.description: const sections = [...product.sections].sort(...) inside ProductSections runs on every render. ProductSections is a client component that receives product as a prop — if any parent re-renders, this sort re-runs.
issues.impact: Minor — product detail pages are relatively static; memoization avoids the allocation on incidental re-renders.

issues.title: Per-row receipt URL fetch inside mapRowToOrder called via Promise.all
issues.description: mapRowToOrder calls getReceiptUrl individually for each row. While these calls are made in parallel via Promise.all (lines 192-202), each call creates a separate signed URL request to Supabase Storage. The buyer-orders path (orderQueries.ts) already pre-batches receipt paths before mapping. Consider the same approach here to keep patterns consistent and avoid creating N individual storage API calls.
issues.suggestedFix: Pre-collect receipt paths and batch them (like in orderQueries.ts lines 47-58), then pass the resolved URL into the mapping step.
issues.impact: Moderate when the received-orders list is long. Supabase Storage signed URLs are network calls; batching reduces round-trip overhead.
issues.note: The existing orderQueries.ts already shows the correct batching pattern as a reference.

issues.title: No dynamic() imports used anywhere in apps
issues.description: No files in apps/ use Next.js dynamic() for code-splitting heavy components. The InlineEditor, ImageGallery, TemplateEditor, and IconPicker are all statically imported. These are moderately complex components that are not always rendered on initial page load.
issues.suggestedFix: Consider using dynamic(() => import('./InlineEditor'), { ssr: false }) for the studio InlineEditor and admin TemplateEditor, which include heavyweight dependencies (drag-and-drop, icon pickers) and are only rendered on user interaction.
issues.impact: Moderate — reduces initial bundle for seller studio and admin pages, improving first-load performance.

issues.title: used for OAuth avatar — intentional and documented
issues.description: An element is used for the OAuth avatar URL (profile.avatar_url). The file has an eslint-disable comment explaining that next/image optimization is not needed for external OAuth avatars. This is correct: next/image requires the external domain to be whitelisted in next.config, and OAuth provider URLs are not predictable.
issues.impact: None — this is intentional and correctly documented. Listed for completeness only.

patterns.title: Good pattern: CartContext is fully memoized
patterns.description: apps/store/src/features/cart/application/CartContext.tsx correctly uses useCallback for all mutation handlers and wraps the context value in useMemo. This is the reference pattern for context providers.

patterns.title: Good pattern: orderQueries.ts pre-batches signed URLs
patterns.description: apps/payments/src/features/orders/infrastructure/orderQueries.ts (lines 47-58) collects all receipt paths, resolves them in a single Promise.all, then maps results — avoiding N individual storage API calls. This is the reference pattern for bulk signed URL resolution.

patterns.title: Good pattern: useMemo used correctly in ProductCatalogPage and ProductGrid
patterns.description: ProductCatalogPage wraps the client-side filter computation in useMemo with correct dependencies. ProductGrid wraps grid-order computation in useMemo. These are well-done.
